forecast-period.text: A low pressure system is expected to move slowly eastwards over waters to the south of the state and will extend a trough over southern parts of SA today. The low will then move over Bass Strait later today. A ridge will extend across SA from the west on Tuesday and a cold front will move across the south later Wednesday. A high pressure system will become established south of WA on Thursday and will extend a ridge over western SA.
Wind and wave forecasts are averages. Wind gusts can be 40 per cent stronger than the forecast, and stronger still in squalls and thunderstorms. Maximum waves can be twice the forecast height.
For latest warnings go to www.bom.gov.au, subscribe to RSS feeds, call 1300 659 210* or listen for warnings on relevant TV and radio broadcasts.
* Calls to 1300 numbers cost around 27.5c incl. GST, higher from mobiles or public phones. Marine forecasts are also available on VHF and HF radio. View the radio schedule on www.bom.gov.au/marine
Check the latest Coastal Waters Forecasts for information on wind, wave and weather conditions for neighbouring coastal zones.

area.description: Lower South East Coast: Cape Jaffa to SA-VIC Border

forecast-period.warning-summary: Strong Wind Warning for Monday for Lower South East Coast
forecast-period.text: Cloudy. 90% chance of showers. The chance of a thunderstorm in the late morning and afternoon.
Southwesterly 15 to 20 knots turning west to northwesterly during the afternoon and evening.
1.5 to 2.5 metres, decreasing to 1 metre during the morning.
West to northwesterly 15 to 25 knots turning southwesterly 25 to 30 knots during the day.
1 to 2 metres, increasing to 2 to 3 metres during the afternoon or evening.
Southwesterly 2.5 to 3 metres, increasing to 3 to 4 metres during the evening.
Cloudy. Near 100% chance of showers, with possible small hail in the afternoon and evening.
